priorities.check_method: S91 P1 (1cd7e3b): G2-PROMO Phase 1 visual-only. Dot->Square=Anchor (fx.anchor) + Line->Circle=Spindle (fx.spindle) appended to combos.ts MAGICAL (isMagical:true, MID/1.0, FORWARD keys only -> reversed pairs stay functional by §V.1; verified in the promotion test). 2 stroke-only STATIC silhouettes in axisAligned.ts (drawAnchor=shaft+stock crossbar+2 flukes=4 strokes; drawSpindle=shaft+2 sin-envelope bows=3 strokes), both with the len<1 drawDefaultLine degenerate guard, no p.tick ref -> joined STATIC_SILHOUETTES (tick-invariance lock green). Dispatch (bondVisualRenderer switch) + barrel (index.ts) wired. The 8x magic income premium (scoring.ts:94/117 isMagical branch), the NEW-COMBO toast (comboDiscovery.ts:37 isMagical gate) and the Combos N/14 HUD (ui.ts:163 MAGIC_12_KEYS.length) ALL auto-follow -> ZERO new scoring/wire/save code. Option A rebalance: PHASE_1_WIN_SCORE 210->630, SCORE_TIER_STEP 70->210 (scoring.test.ts:330-331 invariant 630/210=3, 630%210=0 green; HUNTER_TRIGGER auto-scales to 472). LOCKED_DECISIONS sect.6 amended Magic-12->14 + win-score version note (naming gate satisfied: user 'go' accepted the default plan naming Anchor/Spindle). EXECUTION DISCOVERY the PDR/audit MISSED: the 3x SCORE_TIER_STEP jump blew the FIXED 20000-tick guard on 5 tier-pulse accrual loops (session10:336/374/427 + session13:326/350) -- at complexity 13 the 2-band target (420) needs ~38.8k ticks > 20000; caught during the pre-edit grep sweep by modeling ticks-to-band, fixed by scaling the guards to 120000 (intent-preserving, tests still assert >=target + exact tier sequence). The WIN-reaching loops were verified SAFE (gameState.test:52 has a 100000 guard; scoring.test:315 pre-seeds at WIN-0.001). RALPH:PATROL inline: spec-conformant to the PDR + DELTA. Adversarial CHECK (3-lens refute-first, ran inline after the workflow's agents died on an org spend-limit not findings): (1) BALANCE re-derived CLEAN against live constants; (2) DESYNC/WIRE-SAVE CLEAN -- scoring is host-only (clients mirror scoreByPlayer, S84 distributed-pipeline precedent), bond A->B order is serialized so lookupCombo is identical host/client/replay, discoveredCombos serializes the KEY STRINGS BY VALUE (save.ts:525-526, sorted) not indices so 2 new keys are purely additive (schemaVersion stays 1, no PROTOCOL_VERSION bump), the new silhouettes are render-only/no synced state; (3) TEST-NET CLEAN -- green suite empirically confirms no silent weakening + the static-tick lock holds, but found doc-drift (the S90 lesson) -> swept 12->14 across combos.test/comboDiscovery/worldTypes/world/gameMode/ui/bondCommit/silhouettes comments + BACKLOG ROADMAP/HISTORY. tsc 0; vitest 1433/1433; bundle 548.3 KiB < 550. VISUAL/FEEL GAP: the in-match Anchor/Spindle silhouettes + the rebalanced pacing are the user playtest (Pixi MP not harness-drivable).

reflexion_entries_to_archive: P1 #recover-an-orphaned-workflow-from-its-journal-dont-rerun: the G2-PROMO design workflow (5 map + 3 design + synthesis + audit = 10 agents) ran to COMPLETION but a SessionStart:resume tore down the detached process at the finish line, so the completion notification never delivered and the task fell off the list -- it LOOKED like a failure. Every agent result was journaled to disk (journal.jsonl, 10 'result' records). I recovered the full PDR (the synthesis string) + the READY audit (the dict) directly from the journal with a tiny python extractor -- zero re-run, zero token waste. LESSON: a background workflow that 'failed' on a session-resume almost certainly finished its agents; read journal.jsonl in the workflow transcript dir and pull the cached results before spending tokens to re-run. (Bonus trap: the PDR string had a Unicode arrow that crashed a naive cp1252 python print -- write to a utf-8 file, don't print.)

P1 #a-relative-test-still-breaks-on-a-fixed-iteration-budget: the PDR + the design-phase audit both asserted only scoring.test.ts:330-331 needed care for the win-score rebalance. WRONG -- tripling SCORE_TIER_STEP (70->210) silently broke 5 tier-pulse tests (session10 x3 / session13 x2). The tests are RELATIVE (they accrue until scoreProgress >= SCORE_TIER_STEP), so they're semantically correct, BUT they cap iteration at a FIXED 20000 ticks; at complexity 13 the 2-band target (420) needs ~38.8k ticks, blowing the guard. I caught it during the pre-edit grep sweep for PHASE_1_WIN_SCORE/SCORE_TIER_STEP consumers by actually MODELING ticks-to-band (income = 0.05*complexity/PHYSICS_HZ). LESSON: when a rebalance scales a constant N x, grep for every FIXED-iteration accrual loop keyed to that constant and re-derive whether the loop budget still reaches the (now N x larger) target -- a relative assertion does not protect a fixed iteration cap. Bumped to 120000 (intent-preserving).

P1 #verify-the-serialization-SHAPE-not-just-the-no-bump-claim: the desync-safe claim hinged on whether discoveredCombos serializes by VALUE (key strings) or by INDEX into a 12-element MAGIC_12_KEYS array -- the latter would have made +2 combos shift indices and silently corrupt cross-version saves/mirrors. Rather than trust the PDR's 'additive-optional, no bump', I READ save.ts:525-526 and confirmed it emits [...world.discoveredCombos].sort() -- the key STRINGS by value, rebuilt into a Set on rehydrate (save.ts:722). So 2 new keys are purely additive data; schemaVersion stays 1, no PROTOCOL_VERSION bump. LESSON: for a 'this is wire/save-safe' claim, verify the actual SERIALIZATION SHAPE (by-value vs by-index/by-count) in the serializer, not just the no-bump assertion -- an index/count-based encoding is the failure mode a count change would trip.

AUDIT #passing-green-gates-still-warrant-the-doc-drift-sweep: tsc 0 + vitest 1433/1433 + bundle green said nothing about the 8 stale 'magic-12'/'Combos N/12'/'12 magical + 24 functional' CURRENT-STATE comments (comboDiscovery/worldTypes/world/gameMode/ui/bondCommit/silhouettes/combos.test) and the BACKLOG ROADMAP still listing PROMO as DEFERRED -- exactly the S90 #ship-doesnt-mean-skip-the-doc-drift-sweep lesson recurring. Swept all current-state count comments 12->14 (left the HISTORICAL session-tagged S7/S19/S20 ones -- editing those would be revisionist) + marked PROMO shipped in the ROADMAP + added the S91 SESSION HISTORY entry. LESSON: after a count/identity change, grep the literal old count across src + docs and fix every PRESENT-TENSE claim the same session; distinguish current-state drift (fix) from dated history (leave).

CHECK #a-doc-sweep-can-MANUFACTURE-a-false-claim: my own 12->14 doc-drift sweep blindly swapped bondCommit.ts:5 from '12 magic + 24 functional' to '14 magic + 22 functional' -- but the BOND_COMMIT commit-pop switch still dispatches only 12 magic combos (Anchor/Spindle correctly fall to the default ring pop; bespoke commit-flair is Phase-2 scope). So the sweep turned a stale-but-harmless comment into an actively FALSE one. The 3-agent adversarial CHECK's test-net lens caught it (MED) by reading the SWITCH, not the comment. Reworded to state the 12-bespoke / 24-default-ring split + documented the deferral; left the dated S7/S19/S20 history. LESSON: a count-swap doc sweep is only safe where the number describes DATA that actually changed -- when the number describes CODE (a dispatch arity, a hardcoded test list), verify the code changed too, or you manufacture a false claim worse than the stale one. Sweep by reading the referent, not by find-replacing the digit.
